behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is the handoff from history-prep logic into the LLM request payload, not just isolated list slicing. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network requirement, no dependence on model judgment, no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior is about payload composition before the call, not real HTTP/subprocess behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no assertion depends on LLM output quality or multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chatbot service + prompt/history builder + stub LLM client; send >10-message history and assert the outbound LLM request contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is response relevance/quality, which depends on real model behavior and can vary; stubs would always hide this failure. MULTI-STEP: single model-evaluation target, so Workflow not required. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text ('addresses the customer's question' vs 'generic/off-topic'), which is model-judgment content, not wrapper plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt+history through the production model path and evaluate whether answers remain on-topic for a curated relevance set (with acceptance thresholds over repeated runs).

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: 'correctly identifies too formal' is a model judgment task; mocking tone classification would mask real misclassification. Orchestration is present, but one model-governed decision drives the branch, so Agentic is sufficient. Rejection gate: Workflow considered, but behavior does not require validating quality across multiple model decisions in sequence; it requires one judgment plus branch trigger.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic casual-tier conversations through real tone-check logic/model; verify overly formal drafts are flagged and regeneration path is invoked, across repeated trials to capture model variance.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in module interaction between conversation pipeline and persistence schema/transaction boundaries. REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is enough; no real external network/OS primitive is required. LLM-DEP: assertions are about stored records/timestamps, not model quality. Rejection gate: System disqualified because behavior text is local persistence correctness, reproducible with in-process app + test DB.
behaviors.test_description: Execute conversation flow against a real local test database and assert all exchange records (initial + regenerations) are written atomically with correct timestamp fields and ordering.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is state handoff/orchestration across modules (tone branch, response selection, delivery path). LLM-DEP: behavior can be tested by forcing the trigger branch and asserting emitted payload, without depending on model output quality. MULTI-STEP: sequence exists, but correctness target is replacement logic, not model decision quality, so Workflow is not strictly required. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow were considered but disqualified because behavior text focuses on visibility and replacement ('customer never sees rejected draft'), which is deterministic pipeline behavior once trigger is true.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service modules with stubbed model outputs where tone trigger is forced true; assert only regenerated response is returned/emitted to the client and rejected draft is absent from customer-facing channel while still logged as internal attempt if applicable.
